review_highlights.comment: One of the best & passionate teachers I've had! The night before our final, she stayed up until late at night to answer student's questions on Piazza. Prof. Shusterman is a really kind person and is receptive to questions. She's young and lively and definitely knows what she's talking about when it comes to Chemistry. While I am definitely not the best at the subject, I still have no ill will towards her because she's very nice. On all of her Piazza posts and emails, she puts smiley faces :)

Professor Shusterman is a Wonderful chem professor! Her lectures are clear and relatively interesting and she offers a lot fo resources for her students to do well. The exams aren't impossible but you definitely need to study for them! She provides study worksheets, practice exams, and always answers her piazza questions for her students.

LIVE LAUGH LOVE DR.S ! Her lectures are concise and clear, and her office hours are very helpful; she's super receptive to any and all questions. Chemistry is hard and the exams aren't easy (three midterms), but if you watch the lectures and complete all the extra practice sheets/exams she provides, you will do well.

Professor Shusterman is an excellent lecturer and I would I highly reccommend taking courses with her. I've never met such a caring professor like her. Now keep in mind Chem 1A has 1500 students, so she doesn't really know everyone. During office hours she takes her time learning your name and addresses you by name. She really is amazing.

Take Ron's War class. You'll learn a lot. Grading on the midterm and final are fair. Section attendance is worth 20% of the grade. Ron is an amazing prof who is entertaining, caring, and is excellent at explaining concepts. Go to his office hours!! He genuinely cares about each of his students.

Stellar man.  Tries hard to be fair and profesional.  No wonder Muslim and Jewish students who are against or for Israel/Palestine sit in the class together.  I guess that's why.  Respect.  Also lectures are fun and exciting.

Great Class, professor gives 3 additional points for writing a film review, so many people got an A in the class. The midterm and final are 2 essays and a few ID questions. analysis.name: Adrian Lee

Chill guy but terrible lecturer. Seems to be very knowledgeable and intelligent but he can't teach. Professor Salim is amazing! He is able to engage the students with the materials and create a welcoming environment within the class. A's for All? More like A's for None!

Worst course experience at Berkeley. Took the course because it was supposed to be computational but the professor made theory-based. Learned nothing useful for any job. analysis.name: Shobhana Murali Stoyanov

review_highlights.comment: Shobhana is an awful professor who doesn't know how to teach. 350 characters is not enough to capture my hate for this Professor.

The professor is disorganized and insists on cold calling, which many students find unproductive and uncomfortable. This class made me reconsider my double major, especially during a tough semester with CS 61B and Data 100.

There are many great professors at Berkeley, but unfortunately, Shobhana isn't one of them. At first, I thought she was a fantastic lecturer, but as the semester went on, I realized the class had no structure. She jumps around from topic to topic and it becomes very hard to follow. I definitely recommend taking stat 20 with another professor.

Extremely disorganized, most of the lectures are not relevant to homework. Done most of the studying from textbook and sections. Doesn't post any practice exams for the final, and no discussion sheets, almost impossible to pass the class as she gives no support. Would avoid at ALL COSTS.

analysis.name: Nicholas Mathew

review_highlights.comment: best class I've taken at Berkeley. Mathew is a riot: razor sharp and  opinionated, totally changed how I think about classical music. the few times you're bored, it's kinda nice just to watch him... ;)

Mathew is a born lecturer. Easily the most hilarious and well-spoken professor I have ever had. SO in touch with his audience (this is rare in lectures) and always open to chat. In all my years at Cal (and believe me, it's been too many...) I've never had a professor as eloquent or as engaging as Prof. Mathew. TAKE HIS CLASSES. :) Ladies, he's kind of a dreamboat.

One of the best professors I have ever had.  Funny, clear, passionate about the subject matter, concise, friendly and approachable.  Tests were fair, papers were short and lectures were fun... He's an excellent lecturer, no doubt about it. My issue was the fact that he seemed to heavily dislike me on a personal level. Professor Robak is probably one of the worst present at Cal. She refuses to curve and makes her tests incredibly hard, theyre not up to par with her lectures at all or the psets/practice tests. Success tips: If you can understand how she writes/organizes her notes, do iClicker in bed and skip lecture. Each week, spend 4 hours learning/reading from her posted lecture notes, and 8 hours tackling her *2* psets for the week. I made an account here just so I could give my review of Dr. Robak. Overall, she is friendly and approachable. Final exam heavy; I went from A to C this week.

She's a great professor! Super helpful and super kind. Stay on top of the material, and you'll do well. Doesn't deserve any negative feedback. She works hard and knows her stuff extremely well. Definitely worth enrolling in her class

Professor Nadeem is an amazing instructor! The course was well-structured and the projects we do are directly applicable to the real-world. Exams are pretty easy too - just attend lecture. Professor Nadeem's marketing class was really fun! It's relatively easy and you just have to follow the study guide for exams. Professor Nadeem truly cares about his students and takes time to get to know everyone. He keeps the class engaging through group discussions and participation. analysis.name: Nicholas Paige
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 39 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Great professor! Loves interacting with students during class, makes an effort to learn people's names. 2 midterms + final exam, 6 short writes (which are basically one-page essays, which you can resubmit if you get a bad grade), and 3 readings per week that you must annotate for a grade. Recorded every lecture, was super accommodating.

Professor Paige is incredibly engaging and wants the best for students. He's super lenient with his grading of the short writes (unless you use AI), but be prepared to actually study for the exams. The class itself was super interesting and honestly one of the best ones I've taken so far. It made me have a whole new appreciation for Paris.

Took this class Fall 24 for Art and Lit breadth and it ended up being one of my favorite classes I've taken thus far at Cal! Prof Paige was super engaging and I always enjoyed going to lecture. You'll do just fine if you do the readings and study appropriately for midterms. Maybe not the easiest breadth out there, but I loved it!

I'd say that making a study guide of the recorded lectures is the best way to go with this class, as well as using flashcards or something similar to remember certain concepts. The tests are very lecture heavy material wise and they also borrow some from the readings/films. The professor is fantastic!

Although Professor Paige is friendly, if you want a low-commitment AC course to fulfill the requirement, avoid this one. There was an excessive amount of lengthy and confusing readings, all of which were assigned via Perusall. Moreover, the exams cover bizarrely specific details from the lectures that I never would have considered reviewing.

analysis.name: Maura Nolan
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty

Utterly awesome. Even if you have no interest in medieval literature, take a class from her. Absolutely the best professor in the English Department. She made difficult texts relatable to the everyday person. Extremely approachable in and out of the classroom. I took 180E as an elective because she was co-teaching it. Don't pass on the opportunity to take a class with her if you can. You won't regret it.

She's amazing. She made medieval and early modern literature interesting and compelling simply because of her own enthusiasm for it. She always illuminated the material and made me wish I had the time to go back and re-read it. Completely fair grading. Very, very nice in office hours. Highly, highly recommended.

Quite disorganized but an easy grader. Only takes attendance when he remembers to, which is rare

I acknowledge this is largely out of his control, but I found his accent made lectures somewhat difficult to understand. Plus, his grading criteria and attendance policy is unclear (we don't know our attendance score ever!), in addition to the GSI's for the course being ok. tbh the subject matter is good but there are better R1B's out there

analysis.name: Doris Tsao
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This is for Neu 100B. The lectures go all over the place. The slides are complex, and she goes off on tangents. Information overload constantly, and there is never any indication of what specific things we should study, she just assumes we will absorb her slides in their entirety. Easily the worst experience I've ever had learning neuroscience.
her lectures are disorganized and she has trouble explaining difficult concepts clearly. lots of tagents and information that does not help the student and only further confuses them

Shows up late a lot, disorganized, doesn't articulate very well, and loves using very abstract odd-angle fMRI diagrams then talking about these blobs for 10 minutes while 100 students gaze on uncomprehending. I wish somebody else, anyone else, was teaching this unit.
The basis for much of what we were learning was not provided. A lot of photos on the slides and not nearly enough text, making it difficult to go back and study at times unless you write down a lot.

Bad professor and uncaring person. Lectures didn't cover majority of exam material and explanations of topics she should have been highly knowledgable in lacked all necessary details. Has no compassion for students that are ill and doesn't respond to student concerns over email or on discussion boards in a timely manner.

analysis.name: Edward Tyerman
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Amazing professor who cares about what he is teaching, he will go on tangents at times but it adds to the class and makes it better lowkey.

Brilliant man! So caring and passionate. Often went on tangents, but they were fascinating and thrilling. Much more of a seminar than a lecture-heavy class, encourages participation. Really interesting reading list. Two papers, take home midterm and final. Spends lots of time grading but gives incredibly thorough feedback. Highly highly recommend!

This was by far my favorite class at UC Berkeley. We covered some authors that I would have never read if it wasn't in this course. Edward encourages everyone to participate (more a seminar than a lecture) and always gives brilliant feedback. The movies/books/ songs/secondary readings are really interesting; I was amazed by the sources chosen!

This was one of m favorite classes I've taken so far. The class relied heavy on participation. He always made every comment feel like it brought something to the conversation. He often would go on tangents, but it was filled with important information and other context relating to the readings. He was a great professor and highly recommend!

analysis.name: Gaston Sanchez Trujillo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 64 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: It's just weird... He looks very nice in person except being a boring lecturer. However, his policy and curve are ridiculous. I can't relate the evil policy and grading with a person who seems to be pretty nice.......

He is not a good option for this class. Although its 3 units and the assignments do not seem like much work, the grading is vague and inconsistent, costing many points. He also blames any trouble on the students even when its clearly staff fault. I had near 100 in assignments but still got a mediocre grade because of his class is structured.
Very unclear with his rubrics and gives boring assignments. His quizzes focus on  R/RMD syntax rather than actual programming or data processing. Oh, and he also doesn't curve.
Literally the worst professor I have ever had. Absolutely useless lectures and a horrible grading scale. He is one of the few people I actively dislike

Mandatory lectures that are utterly useless. If attendance wasn't mandatory, you could get by without ever attending lecture or doing any reading. Assignments are easy, but points are docked for trivial mistakes even if it's obvious that you understand the content being taught. The class isn't very useful or meaningful, but Sanchez makes it worse

analysis.name: Tabitha Kanogo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She is literally the sweetest and most caring professor with some light notes of humor. She lectures very well and is extremely knowledgable; although they can be wordy and hard to sit through sometimes, but overall she was amazing. Coursework is very light especially if you have a great GSI. Read the books as they will help a lot on the tests.

She is so sweet and if you have a good GSI that's the cherry on top! Lectures are very entertaining and easy to follow. Class has one essay and mostly consists of readings and discussion work. I would read the books for sure as they'll help a lot on the midterms and the final!

She was so smart and interesting so definitely go to lectures. The slides are hardly any info and if they did, it didn't make sense til she explained it. Discs were so helpful and low maintenance. A midterm, one essay, and the final is all you're graded on besides disc attendance. Lots of reading but you don't always have to do it to understand.

Took her Freshman Seminar on the Apartheid and it was terribly boring. Long and droning lectures. She spends most of class discussing what we can learn in other courses. Perhaps her non-seminar classes are more interesting.

the professor is excellent, especially on one-one basis. The GSI, Jeff is terrible. He is very biased and only is helpful towards female students. He should like become a bouncer and hit on girls there.

analysis.name: Tehmina Khan
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Tehmina is the best writing teacher I've ever had. Every class she gives us about 15 minutes for meditation and free writing, so that we can have a quiet moment to really slow down and get into your thoughts. In her class, you can't just stay surface-level with people, you actually get to know each other and engage in real conversations.

She is a really great teacher and genuinely cares about her students' success. Her essays can be challenging because they really force you to think, and her prompts are far from typical. If you genuinely like the subject of the class, you will definitely enjoy the readings. Make sure not to slack off, as it can be very easy to fall behind.

If you have the option to take R1A with Tehmina Khan, definitely do it. The skills that I have learned from this class, as well as the people that I have met definitely amazed me. While it isn't uncommon to get an A- or better in this course, be prepared to write a lot. My biggest piece of advice for this class is to show up and do your reading.

Tehmina is one of the sweetest professors I've had, who cares deeply about what she teaches, social issues, and her students. The readings are engaging and the classroom is interactive with everyone in a small classroom setting. If your having any trouble, just let her know and she'll do what she can to help:)

Really good Professor. I had Tehmina for my R1A "All About Love" her class was super engaging, and it was awesome to have such a small class room. she was always there if you needed help. She also is very caring and sweet. she would always bring Mango's to class for everyone to enjoy. Also one major key point is to stay consistent.

analysis.name: Uri Korisky
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: he is a nice guy and passionate about the subject. knowledgeable too, I like him overall but he seems like OBSESSED with ai and uses it a lot which is off-putting. also the forced class-wide debates are controversial and spooky

Took this class over the summer, and I felt like it was run fully by AI... every email, lecture, and assignment was almost fully written by Chat GPT, and the entire course grading was made up of 60% assignments, 15% midterm, and 25% final. Not a hard class by any means, but honestly wouldn't take it if it weren't fully online.

Attendance mandatory for 5 lectures, additional attendance is ec. Pretty passionate about his classes, has dry humor that shows through in the slides; makes his long lectures better. Open to questions and feedback outside of class time. Test-wise, only 1 midterm + 1 final and both were not too bad. Weird grading bins (95+ for an A) but doable.

uri is a funny guy and always kept it real. i appreciated all the transparency within the course. they can be slow on grading assignments, grade cutoffs are weird (above a 95% is an A), and tests are confusing. takes feedback seriously though and always checks in. class felt really ai heavy. overall, just an okay class experience.

lots of easy ec available (if you >5 lectures, do >10 group projects, etc). lectures were long but he splits them up with break and the last hour being groupwork. overall i dont think the concepts covered were particularly hard, and i felt like i barely did anything but still finished with >100%. is very genuine and cares a lot.

analysis.name: Ussama Makdisi
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very mixed about this professor. On the one hand, very well spoken and gives a lot of good insight on the topic if you have never been educated on it. On the other, lectures can fluctuate wildly from absolutely necessary to attend or a complete waste of time. Quizes were easy, just be prepared to talk about the same thing over and over again.

Makdisi is THE GOAT!!! In a class dealing with such sensitive subject matter, he never failed to offer an open space where students can freely share their ideas and let us come to conclusions ourselves. While the class is long-reading heavy, the important sections are covered in discussion so u should be chilling. Everyone should take this class!!

I do think sometimes the lectures were very repetitive and concepts were overused. At least for history 12 (admittedly lower division), he taught how to study history, especially in the Middle East. I think non-history majors might get more out of his class.

He has passion but I didn't feel like I got much out of his lectures (he has a very soft voice that put me to sleep at times). Cares about his students and grading was not bad. Shout out to the GSIs, learned the most through weekly section.

Makdisi is an incredible historian and I'm grateful to have taken "Palestine and the Palestinians: A Modern History" with him. He assigns engaging readings and is a captivating lecturer. There was a LOT of history to pack in this semester-long class, but Makdisi synthesized immense amounts of information in an understandable format. TAKE THIS CLASS

analysis.name: Ugo Nwokeji
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: No instructions on format for midterms until the night before. Randomly gives you language to memorize on tuesday, test on thursday, then its never spoken of again. Grading is unclear and confusing. No instructions given to gsi's so they never can answer a single question. Classes always start late or are cancelled but godforbid you don't show up

This review is for AFRAM 118. I respectfully believe it is time for Dr. Ugo to retire or pivot in his career. He is not tech savvy, so when his lecture slides supposedly did not work at the onset of the semester, he just gave up. Thus, resulting in a highly ineffective lecture style. He is precariously late with grading and poor at giving feedback.

I do not recommend this class at all!!! the GSI never knew what was going on in that class either. Random quizzes. only mentioned about quiz once and never said what to study for!! mind you he mentioned this quiz a day before it was due!!! no format for midterm or final!!  our essays and finals never got graded and they passed the students!!!

This professor is a mess. We had a map quiz when he never taught geography, we had to memorize words in IGBO for a language quiz worth 5% of our grade, our paper and final were never graded so they never went into the gradebook and the GSIs never knew what was going on in the class. This was a horrible class. Dont recommend it.

He is very endearing.  Cares about students.  HOWEVER, he lacks professionalism.  Some lectures, he did not show up (unexpectedly) or he had seminars to attend instead.  Other times, he could not figure out how to put on powerpoint so we would start 40 minutes into the 90 minute lecture.  Didn't learn too much, but I did learn.  Very light load.

analysis.name: Ken Ueno
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Ken Ueno's class is mostly about him. He once spent 30 minutes on a Facebook argument, assigns readings about himself, and reuses his comp class's material. Uses AI art but calls it his own. No focus on notation, just avant-garde noise. Praise him or make bad music for an easy A. Another prof told me the content he teaches is "useless in practice"

I have no idea why Professor Ken will get a 2/5 here. He's an amazing composer and professor, changing the way I look at the world in a more comprehensive, lenient, and poetic way. He's class is all about his student, I don't know where else will give the opportunity to student to work with 5 world-class musicians and write for them in a semester.

Took his analysis class, felt like absolutely no planning at all, every class he just talks about whatever he's in the mood to talk about. Assignments and quizzes were sporadically given, and the grading felt pretty arbitrary too. He is clearly very knowledgable, but due to his lack of organization for this class, I learned very little from it.

He digresses a lot in class and the entire class feels sort of monotonic and gloomy. He is not the worst kind of teacher you can have, I feel like he is pretty polite, but still can make you a little bit uncomfortable. He focuses on avant-garde kind of music so if that is not your type I would suggest you to go find someone else.

Prof Ken is interesting and knows a lot. However, he's a poor teacher and honestly not sure why he's allowed to teach w/o changing. He just talks randomly without bringing the class along for the ride. He didnt care much. The guest lecturers can be really cool and neat. Also the project compostions are very avant garde.

analysis.name: Hendrik Utzat
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Organized and well-articulated lectures. Able to present the same concept in many different ways. Impeccable lecture notes (color-coded to emphasize important concepts). Kind and accessible during office hours. Problem sets are challenging but doable if you first try to understand the lectures. Very responsive to feedback.
Just a mess. He's nice but he manages to make simple concepts so convoluted. Lectures are all over the place. The hw is really not that bad but I literally hate how he explains things

analysis.name: Umair Khan
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This is the ONE class you need to take before you leave UC Berkeley. It is life-changing and so inspirational. Professor Umair Khan is a one-of-a-kind professor who genuinely cares about teaching and inspiring students. He's funny, inspiring, and cares. It's so amazing to walk into class each week and hear from such amazing people. A MUST TAKE!!

I would leave Umair's class every Wed buzzing. Getting to hear from the founders of companies that I grew up with (GuitarHero, Rotten Tomatoes, Khan Academy, etc). was one of the best experiences I could have had. It showed me starting a company was actually possible, and gave me to playbook to actually go and and start my own company. Take it!!

Do not take this class if you are an creative in any way. The class says it's for everyone, but is so heavily pro-AI to the point where AI generated prompts will beat out all the creative ideas. There are many students who can't do anything but chatGPT. Other than learning about VC terms, this class does not teach you how to actually run a business

Hum 120 is by far the best class I've taken at Cal. Highly recommend it to anyone interested in entrepreneurship. This class builds a solid foundation for anyone interested in building a company. Plus the network Professor Huang and Khan brings to class is truly unique.
This class helped me grow and learn alot of things professor Umair has been supportive throughout. I wish I could take another class with him

analysis.name: Elcin Unal
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She was a good lecturer and is consistent with her OH. I liked that she gave us extra practice questions to study. Her exam questions are super hard tho so watch out! She didn't give us much info about what we should expect on the exams, and she puts a lot of easter egg details on the questions which if you don't notice, you may as well get a 0.

Clear, enthusiastic lecturer. Definitely cares about her teaching quality. Posts amazing, almost word-for-word lecture notes that explain all concepts well, and could substitute for any textbook you'd need. Her midterm was moderately tricky and harshly graded, though, so don't underestimate it on the basis of the quizzes (both "big" and "mini".)

Elcin is an AMAZING professor, one of the best. Everyone loves her and we wish she could teach the entire class! Lectures are very well planned out and the logical flow is on point. She is great at explaining and laying everything out in a simple explanation. Also gives lecture notes and outside resources/practice. Would highly recommend.

The best of the three, although her concepts are also the easiest, review of Mendelian genetics from bio1a. Lectures are very clear and organized, she uses the chalkboard unlike most MCB profs. Exams are similar to practice problems and past exams, but still require thinking. Highly recommend.

One of my favorite professors at Berkeley! Amazing lectures with clear chalk work, she also has very detailed lecture notes uploaded for reference. Very willing to help students both after class and during office hours. 10/10 recommend.

analysis.name: Aleksandra Utiralova
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great professor. The notes she provided were very helpful and made understanding the material much easier. Always happy to answer questions. The lecture pacing was a bit fast so I did have to go and reread the textbook/notes quite a bit. Practice exams are literally just like the real thing. However, the HW is hard and worth a lot of your grade.

She posts her lecture notes online, which I thought were far better than the actual textbook itself. In fact, I pretty much used them as a substitute for the lectures itself (which I could not attend due to a time conflict). Homeworks are tough and exams are fair. Would definitely take another class with Prof. Utiralova in the future.

I learned so much from this class. Utiralova has her lecture notes freely available so you can read ahead and she records her lectures. Homework gives you enough practice to understand concepts but also throws in some tough questions - good balance. Exams are extremely fair, and mirror the practice exams fairly. I would definitely recommend!
the homework was HARD and time consuming but I have a lot of respect for her, she is very nice and smart, after all she went to MIT
Great Professor, her exams are similar to mock

analysis.name: Fyodor Urnov
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 141 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Urnov is my fav professor at Berkeley. i would've loved 2 be taught by him earlier in my undergrad. he's such a sweetheart+mad inspirational. always related course content to the Real World, the past and future. encouraged ALL questions in lec. only thing is he's hard 2 reach out of class (does NOT answer emails rip). genome editing king, 1 mil/10.

Dr. Urvov was by far and away my favourite professor I've had (and I'm a senior!). His section has material that is so new there is no textbook for it. Fyodor is very intentional— he wants students to pay attention and makes lectures unbelievably interesting, letting his humour and passion for the subject come through. Take any class he teaches.

Hilarious professor who will emphasize a) why the material you are learning was important to the past and b) to the future. He keeps the class exciting by updating it with current and relevant research papers. Pretty lively and passionate guy

He's Sarcastic, Bitter, has his 12 old daughter teach a class on the Jonas Brothers, lies about his office hours, loves politics, cancels review sessions, refers all difficult biochem questions to the GSIs, NEVER responds to emails, has little to NO knowledge of biochem, very arrogant, mocks students who care about grades, that about sums it up.GL!

Most inspirational bio 1a prof.  People didn't like the question phrasing on his tests, but that's probably because they didn't study enough.  Honestly, the tests were very very easy if you study the lecture slides. Would definitely recommend.
